name: maskformer

members.name: MaskFormerConfig

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 150, in_channels: int = 3, backbone_layers: tuple[int, int, int, int] = (3, 4, 6, 3), backbone_block: Literal['basic', 'bottleneck'] = 'bottleneck', d_model: int = 256, n_head: int = 8, num_encoder_layers: int = 6, num_decoder_layers: int = 6, dim_feedforward: int = 2048, dropout: float = 0.1, num_queries: int = 100, fpn_out_channels: int = 256)

members.extended:
MaskFormer (Cheng et al., NeurIPS 2021) reformulates semantic segmentation
as mask classification: N learnable queries each predict a class label and
a binary mask.  Predictions are matched to ground-truth segments via
Hungarian matching during training.

parameters.name: dim_feedforward
parameters.description: FFN inner dimension in each transformer layer.

parameters.name: num_queries
parameters.description: Number of learnable object queries N.

notes:
Image → ResNet backbone → [C2, C3, C4, C5]
  → FPN Pixel Decoder → per-pixel embeddings (B, d_model, H/4, W/4)
  → Transformer Decoder (N queries attend to pixel embeddings)
  → Class head: Linear(d_model, num_classes+1) per query
  → Mask head: query-dot-pixel → binary mask per query

seg_logits[b, k, h, w] = sum_n softmax(class_logits)[b,n,k]
                                * sigmoid(mask_logits)[b,n,h,w]
Upsampled to input resolution and argmaxed for final prediction.

members.name: MaskFormerForSemanticSegmentation

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, targets: dict[str, Tensor] | None = None)

parameters.name: targets
parameters.description: Optional dict with `"masks"` (B, H, W) integer masks.

returns.description:
`SemanticSegmentationOutput`:
`logits`: (B, K+1, H, W) per-pixel class logits.
`loss`:   training loss when targets provided.

members.summary: MaskFormer semantic / panoptic segmentation model (Cheng et al., NeurIPS 2021).
members.extended:
Recasts semantic segmentation as **mask classification**: instead of
per-pixel cross-entropy, the model predicts a fixed set of
$N$ (mask, class-probability) pairs, computes the per-pixel
class probability as

$$
p_\mathrm{px}(c) = \sum_{i=1}^{N} p_i(c)\, m_i(\mathrm{px}),
$$
and supervises the set with bipartite (Hungarian) matching to ground-
truth masks.  This unification lets a single architecture handle
semantic, instance, and panoptic segmentation with the same training
objective — the breakthrough that the follow-up Mask2Former extends
with masked attention.

Architecturally: a ResNet backbone -> FPN-style pixel decoder
produces a per-pixel embedding of dimension `d_model`, and a
transformer decoder operates on $N$ query embeddings, attending
to the encoder memory; sibling heads then produce $(N, K+1)$
class logits and $(N, d)$ mask embeddings.  Each query's binary
mask is recovered by a dot product with the pixel embedding.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use `maskformer_resnet50` /
`maskformer_resnet101` for the paper-cited variants
(ADE20K, 150 classes).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.maskformer import maskformer_resnet50
>>> model = maskformer_resnet50()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 512, 512)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B, K + 1, H, W)
(1, 151, 512, 512)
notes:
See Cheng et al., "Per-Pixel Classification is Not All You Need for
Semantic Segmentation", NeurIPS 2021 ([arXiv:2107.06278](https://arxiv.org/abs/2107.06278)).  The total
loss combines a CE term over query class predictions and a per-mask
binary CE + dice term, summed only over the Hungarian-matched query
permutation $\hat{\sigma}$:

$$
\mathcal{L} = \sum_{i=1}^{N}
    \bigl[\mathcal{L}_\mathrm{cls}\bigl(c_i, p_{\hat{\sigma}(i)}\bigr)
    + \mathbb{1}_{c_i \ne \varnothing}\,
      \mathcal{L}_\mathrm{mask}\bigl(m_i, \hat{m}_{\hat{\sigma}(i)}\bigr)
    \bigr].
$$
For semantic-segmentation inference the model collapses queries by
softmax-weighted summation into a standard $(B, K + 1, H, W)$
logit map for direct argmax evaluation.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: pixel_decoder
attributes.description:
FPN-style top-down decoder yielding a per-pixel embedding
$(B, d, H/4, W/4)$.

attributes.name: transformer_decoder
attributes.description:
`num_decoder_layers`-layer transformer decoder applied to the
queries with memory drawn from the backbone's final stage.

attributes.name: class_head
attributes.description:
Per-query $(K + 1)$ classification head (`+1` for the
"no object" class).

attributes.name: mask_embed
attributes.description: Per-query embedding producing the dot-product mask weights.

members.name: maskformer_resnet50
members.summary: MaskFormer with ResNet-50 backbone (Cheng et al., NeurIPS 2021).
members.extended:
Builds a `MaskFormerForSemanticSegmentation` with the paper-cited
ResNet-50 configuration: 100 segmentation queries, a 6-layer transformer
decoder with `d_model = 256`, 8 attention heads, `dim_feedforward =
2048`, and a 256-channel FPN pixel decoder.  Default targets ADE20K
(150 semantic classes); reaches ADE20K validation mIoU of 44.5%
(paper Table 3, MaskFormer-R50 row) — competitive with much heavier
per-pixel baselines.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently ignored.

returns.description:
Segmentation model with the MaskFormer-R50 configuration applied
(or with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.maskformer import maskformer_resnet50
>>> model = maskformer_resnet50(num_classes=21)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 512, 512)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[1]
22
notes:
See Cheng et al., "Per-Pixel Classification is Not All You Need for
Semantic Segmentation", NeurIPS 2021 ([arXiv:2107.06278](https://arxiv.org/abs/2107.06278)).  The
unification of semantic / instance / panoptic under mask
classification is the conceptual key idea.

members.name: maskformer_resnet101
members.summary: MaskFormer with ResNet-101 backbone (Cheng et al., NeurIPS 2021).
members.extended:
Builds a `MaskFormerForSemanticSegmentation` with the same
transformer head as `maskformer_resnet50` but a deeper ResNet-101
backbone (`[3, 4, 23, 3]` bottleneck blocks).  Reaches ADE20K
validation mIoU of 45.5% (paper Table 3, MaskFormer-R101 row).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently ignored.

returns.description:
Segmentation model with the MaskFormer-R101 configuration applied
(or with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.maskformer import maskformer_resnet101
>>> model = maskformer_resnet101()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 512, 512)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[1]
151
notes:
See Cheng et al., "Per-Pixel Classification is Not All You Need for
Semantic Segmentation", NeurIPS 2021 ([arXiv:2107.06278](https://arxiv.org/abs/2107.06278)).  Switching
backbones is the only change versus `maskformer_resnet50`; all
transformer-head hyperparameters are shared.

extended: Paper: "Per-Pixel Classification is Not All You Need for Semantic Segmentation"
citation: Cheng, Bowen, et al. "Per-Pixel Classification is Not All You Need for Semantic Segmentation." Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2021.
theory:
MaskFormer recasts **semantic segmentation** as a
*mask-classification* problem rather than the standard
per-pixel-classification one.  Instead of predicting a
$K$-way softmax at every pixel, the model emits $N$
queries; each query produces a class label and a binary mask,
and the final segmentation is the soft sum



$$
\mathrm{seg}_{k}[h, w] =
    \sum_{n=1}^{N} \mathrm{softmax}(\hat{c}_n)_k \,
    \sigma(\hat{m}_n[h, w]),
$$

where $\hat{c}_n \in \mathbb{R}^{K+1}$ is the per-query
class logit and $\hat{m}_n$ is its binary mask logit.  The
argmax over $k$ yields the predicted label map.

The architecture has three modules:

- **Pixel decoder** — an FPN-like up-sampler that produces a
  per-pixel embedding $\mathcal{E}_{\mathrm{pixel}} \in
  \mathbb{R}^{C_\epsilon \times H/4 \times W/4}$.
- **Transformer decoder** — $N$ learnable queries attend
  to a flattened backbone feature, producing per-query
  embeddings $\mathcal{E}_{\mathrm{query}} \in
  \mathbb{R}^{N \times C_\epsilon}$.
- **Heads** — a linear classifier on each query and a
  dot-product $\mathcal{E}_{\mathrm{query}} \cdot
  \mathcal{E}_{\mathrm{pixel}}$ to produce per-query masks.

Training uses Hungarian matching (as in DETR) between predicted
and ground-truth segments and combines cross-entropy on the
class logits with focal/BCE + Dice losses on the mask logits.

The key insight — that mask classification subsumes per-pixel
classification — also unifies *semantic* and *instance*
segmentation under one model, foreshadowing the more general
Mask2Former.
